public_api_added: func ExecuteSplitAllocateAndWrite(idAlloc *catalog.IDAllocator, cs *catalog.ContentStore, originalContent []byte, plan SplitPlan) (map[string]uint64, error)
downstream_consumers_this_run_does_not_w: engine/split/execute.go (2b.3.2) will consume the returned map[NewPath]fileID to create catalog records with status REDIRECT/SPLIT and redirectTargetIDs
engine/split/execute.go (2b.3.3) will use the same fileIDs to insert new B+Tree topic-path entries
engine/graph/edge_append.go (2b.3.4/2b.3.5) will append SPLIT_SIBLING edges among the returned fileIDs
engine/split/execute.go (2b.3.6) will wrap allocation+write+catalog+btree+graph in one WAL-covered transaction
risk_notes: Highest-risk correctness surface (per AGENT.md / issue milestone): incorrect byte-range slicing would silently corrupt split-off content.

Must not create any catalog record for new fileIDs -- catalog visibility is 2b.3.2's job; IDAllocator.Next() durably reserves the ID but ContentStore.Create/Append/cat.Put are deliberately NOT called here.
Must not regress engine/catalog or existing engine/split tests (2b.1.1-2b.1.3, 2b.2.1-2b.2.2).
